Hello there and welcome to this interview with Igor Kifetz. My name is Phil Ainsworth and I'm very, very excited to be joined today by a rising star in the internet marketing field. You're going to hear his name a lot more as the next few months and years go by. I'm really impressed by the way this guy markets and I'm impressed by a lot of the way he does things. I'm impressed by the way he treats his affiliates and the way he encourages and inspires and motivates his affiliates to promote for him. I'm encouraged by his overall sense of fun and humor and attitude about marketing and just the way he does stuff is absolutely brilliant. I really appreciate it and admire it. So that's why I've got him in the quarter down. I'm going to try and pick his brains full of loads of secrets to share with you. I'm going to try and get as much information out of him and have some fun as well. So I'd really like to welcome you to the quarter. Igor, how are you doing? Feli, are you okay there? Yeah, I'm awesome, especially after that juicy introduction. I gave all the golden nuggets I have. Absolutely. I'm going to put you through the Inquisition Interrogation, mate. You just spilled all your secrets for the people listening. First of all, not everyone listening will have heard of you. We can both appreciate that. Just so they can understand a bit more about you, would you like to tell them your story and tell them how you got started in that marketing and kind of a little bit about yourself and how far you've come? Sure, why not? I started out actually not in internet marketing but in MLM and I met this woman from Australia. I was looking for information on the Make Money Online and I found her, I don't know how you call it, information page or something on the network about the law of attraction. And so she had a video of a guy named Bob Proctor. I know if you've heard of him. He's like a big law of attraction teacher all over the world. Yeah, I think he's in the film The Secret by Proctor, isn't he? Yeah, exactly. But he's also a marketer. I mean a really good one. He has tons of information products and everything. And so he had this video about 10% of people having 90% of all the money in the world. And it got me thinking that why not try, why not make money online? So I contacted this woman on Skype and she gave me a few books, ebooks, read and video courses to watch. For about 8 months I had no results whatsoever. I mean it was a struggle. I had to fight with my family. I refused to go to work, believing that in a market it is the way to go. I mean I had tons of fights with my dad, with my mom, I was living with my parents at the time. I mean it was a tough, tough period. But later I was introduced to a list building and eventually I figured out a few ways to monetize my list with email marketing. And although I had a pretty small list at the time, it took me a lot of time to build it up. I was still able to make some money and give me hope. Now about a year ago, maybe less, I finally, I can honestly say this, I finally cracked the code to making money with email marketing. Now and surprisingly it turned out to be easier than I thought. And so today I can afford to sit around my computer, play video games, send a few emails a day and bam, I'm making couple hundred bucks per day. Which for a guy who lives in Israel is an awesome, awesome salary. My friends are here making four times, if not five times less than I do, working their asses off even on a high end facility such as Intel or whatever. The ones that deal with electronics and sell their technology all over the world. Sure yeah. The guys that have what they call proper jobs. Yeah. They have company cars, they get bonuses and stuff and so they're very, very proud of their incomes. And I can just top that with a few emails per day, which is amazing. And really. Yeah. And so that's exactly what I'm going to try and teach you this year hopefully if Phil's going to ask me the right questions. Do you know what? That's a popular saying. So I'm going to make sure I ask you the right questions. Okay, so thank you for sharing that information with us. So I mean, for anyone listening, it's really good to hear that. To hear that. I mean, you've got to find sources of motivation for yourself, whether it's going to be your kids, your kids, your wife, someone you're trying to make it work for, not only yourself, but rather think of what you're going to do once you get there, the kind of freedom you're going to provide for your family, for yourself. Also, I found one exercise that's really helpful. Every day, before you go to work on your business, reread your goals, your financial goals. So sit down, take five minutes of your time in your bedroom, lock the doors, not the windows. Just think that you're making the kind of money you'd like to make right now. So for example, if your goal is six figures per year, let's say $1,000 per year, just sit, relax, and imagine that right now, today, you're making $100,000 per year. And so what kind of lifestyle can you have, can you enjoy when you're making this kind of money? And I promise you, after just a few minutes of doing this exercise, if you go to work still thinking about that major lifestyle change you're going to experience, you're going to be much more motivated and things will just flow easier. It's going to be silky smooth for you. That's a great piece of advice there. And thank you for sharing that. That's a fantastic piece of advice to write out your own goals, to be 100% clear on what your personal goals are, whatever they may be for yourself, and not just financial ones as well. That can include personal goals to spend more time with your family or whatever. But whatever goals you have, write them out, review them every single day so you're clear in your mind why you're doing this and what you've got at stake, what it would cost you if you didn't achieve your goals. If anyone listening right now that's thinking, oh, I don't go for that psychological rubbish, I don't go for that wishy washy tell yourself and you'll believe it and you'll achieve it kind of mumbo jumbo, anyone listening that's thinking that and who is more into kind of hard science and that, let me tell you about a study. I don't know the exact details, but there was a study taken at Harvard University and they interviewed all the students that went into Harvard University for one year and they questioned them and said, who out of you has written down goals that they review every single day? And I think it was about 5 or 6% of the year's Harvard students, the intake for that year of all the students that were starting at Harvard University, about 5 or 6% had written out goals that they reviewed every day. Anyway, 5 years later, the people that studied doing this experiment, they went back to the year group 5 years later and they asked people, what's your annual earnings? How much money do you make and what's your current wealth? And I don't remember the exact figure, but it was something like 95% of the total wealth of the whole group, of the whole thousand students that had been Harvard University 5 years before, 95% of the wealth was being shared by the 5 or 6% that had had the goals for the 5 years and written, that I'd said that 5 years before, yes, I write my goals, I look at them every day, 95% of all the wealth by the group was by those guys. I don't know the exact deeds of the study. My friend, Dan, who is another marketer, told me about this study and there's some proof right there. For anyone listening that's thinking, oh yeah, that kind of psychological believe-in-you-will-achieve kind of mentality, I don't believe in it. So if somebody is just starting in the IM world, they don't know what they're doing and they haven't got a clue, they've heard so many things, they've heard all these acronyms SEO and PPC and article marketing and affiliate marketing and all these different ways of making money, MLM, they've heard of that as well, what's the easiest and quickest way for someone to get started in the IM world so they can start seeing some results and build from that? What should they do first? If you were to ask me this question about a couple of months ago, I'd probably tell you affiliate marketing. Right, okay. No, and you shed all the doubt. However, today, I must say that the first thing you've got to do is create your own product, whether free or paid, I don't care. You have to have your own product for a few reasons. First, you can sell it and keep 100% of the profits. That's an all-brainer. Second, it gives you credibility, authority, but most importantly, it gives you leverage, which you can use to score joint ventures, get other people promoting for you, doing all the traffic generation for you while we get to keep the customers and build your business. Now, again, it also goes back to email marketing and list building, why? Because you cannot build a list without having something to give away or sell. The easiest way to find a product if you don't know what you're doing right now, there are a few. One, you can go and hire someone to do it for you. Two, if you have a mentor or someone who's looking over at your work, you can speak to this person and ask to create this product for you or to help you do the research and just write it up yourself or shoot the video yourself. Now, if you really have no idea what you're doing right now, then look up Term, PLR, and MRR, which means private label rights and master recent rights. Now, these are products, ready-made products that you can use and resell as your own products. Now, you can find them ready-made products with done sales pages, with done squeeze pages, with done articles, etc., for as low as $10 to $20. You can really get a whole business in a box for as little as $10 to $20 and start using it. All that you have to do next is just get a domain name and hosting account and upload this to your site and start driving traffic to it. So you mentioned a couple of minutes back about creating, for people listening right now, they create their own product, which they can then use as leverage. They can either give it away for free or they can sell it. Okay. Now obviously, if they give it away for free, they're going to create a list of prospects. And if they sell it, they're going to create a list of buyers. Now I know that you wanted to tell people about the important differences between having a list of prospects and having a list of buyers, or of course having both. But why is it so important for people listening right now? Why is it so important that they distinguish between prospects and buyers? Yeah. Thanks for reminding me. But to explain this a little bit further, I need to go step back. When you build a list, the first thing you do is you bribe someone to join your list. And so you give them something for free, whether an e-book or a video or an audio or something, or a software maybe. They join your list for free and you give them the freebie that you promised. Now the next step is to hopefully turn this prospect who was looking for free stuff, which in the industry we like to call a freebie seekers, right? To turn this guy or gal into a buyer. Meaning that you want to get them to hopefully buy one or maybe more of your products. And then that's not just to make money right away. But to actually build another list of buyers. Now a list of buyers, it's been told and I've experienced this, sorry, on my own neck, is ten times more profitable than a list of freebie seekers. Now it means that, for example, if you have a thousand freebie seekers list and only one hard buyer list, you can make more money with your 100 buyers than you can ever make with the 1000 freebie seekers. It happens because these people have already proved they can buy stuff. They can afford it. They want to learn. I mean they're not afraid of being scammed or whatever. They don't have any wicked thoughts about sharing their credit confirmation on the line and so on. And so you can actually make tons more money, as I've said, approximately ten tons more money from these lists than from a freebie seeker list. And another very, very important reason is because later in this year we're going to share a traffic generation strategy that's completely, I mean it's so controversial, I don't even know how to call it. But you will need your freebie seeker list for that traffic generation method while you will be keeping your buyer list close to your chest and you won't be just exploiting them or whatever, whatever you want to call it. I don't want you to think that we're here to exploit someone. No, not at all. I mean in terms of traffic generation, which is also a big, big problem for lots of beginners, especially the ones who are on a shoestring budget. I mean this is really important. So stay tuned. Excellent. Well, we all have a bit of controversy. So I'm sure everyone's looking forward to hearing that. So we'll definitely come back to that. It's interesting that you just mentioned about traffic being one of the key problems that newbies have. I'll just sidestep for two seconds. But I have a belief that most people, like 99% of people, especially newbies, they believe that their one problem is traffic and they believe that if they had more traffic, they'd make a lot more money. But I would say that traffic isn't the problem for most newbies. The biggest problem is either they don't have their own product to sell or they don't have a product that converts very well. And I mean it's great. You can have all the traffic in the world. But if you haven't got your own product to sell and if it doesn't convert, then that traffic is not going to bring you any money. So that's just something that I think in terms of... And secondly, does it convert if I have? So that's just a bit of a thought there. So anyway, sorry to get sidetracked there, just something I wanted to add. So now you've explained to us the importance of having this prospect list separated from the buyer's one because a buyer's list is ten times more powerful. How can everyone listening right now, how can they separate the two lists and maybe make themselves some money while they're doing that? Well, here's how. Here's what you do. Basically, when someone joins your list, you have them opt-in into a special opt-in form which you get from Aweber or GetResponse or whatever the RS1R service that you're using right now. Now, after they've opted-in into your list and your free list, you give them the free beat and immediately you offer them to purchase your paid product. Now you might be wondering how can you do that if they just got your stuff for free? Now here's what something most people don't get and pretty much are afraid of. You need to get your product that you're going to be selling, right, whether it's an e-book or a video or whatever. You need to take the best, I mean the greatest tip of all that you're given away in that paid product for money and give it away for free. One, that will get them to join your list and two, that will build the most important factor or maybe ingredient in your whole marketing system. That is trust because on the internet, trust is a problem. By giving away your best tip for free to these guys, you immediately generate trust with them. Now it's the perfect time to tell them, well, if you like this, if you like my free stuff, I highly recommend checking out my course, my system, my complete guide, my step-by-step blueprint to XYZ and you send them to the sales page, to the paid product. Now hopefully they will buy if your sales page converts and you have an IC cover and whatever. Now when they buy immediately after they pay, you don't send them to the download page like it used to do a few years back. It was tradition just, they buy, you send them to the download page, no questions. Now you send them to a special opt-in page which is buyers only opt-in page. And so you make them opt-in once again, of course you can offer and you should offer a few unadvertised free bonuses in exchange for this opt-in. And you get them on a special buyer list just to separate these folks from your regular opt-ins. Now this is just a special neat trick that will help you identify or rather help those people to identify themselves as buyers and build that buyer list up while selling your products. Now you can't do that if you have an affiliate product and that's why I kind of made an emphasis at the beginning of this interview that you need your own product to build up that buyer list because that is going to be probably the most profitable of all asset to your business. Another one month from now or one year or ten years, that's going to be the thing that makes you the most fun. Absolutely. I agree with you 100% Igor. I mean I've, I'm just touching a couple of things you said there actually. So you mentioned that the best way to be able to sell your product was to take the very best information that you had and put that into your freebie so that people could tell by the freebie just how good the information that you have is. Now I know that I'm going to guess that a few people listening right now, maybe a lot of people listening right now are going to be thinking, but that doesn't make sense. If I give away my best information for free, nobody's going to buy the products, right? I know that's what a few people are going to be thinking and that's what I used to think as well. I'm going to answer the friend of mine and explain it to me and I'll just quickly explain so people can understand if they're thinking. But that's crazy. If you give away your best stuff for free, no one's going to buy the other stuff, right? If you're thinking that, think of a big music act like Katy Perry or Lady Gaga. She's probably the most famous pop star in the world, Lady Gaga. Now when Lady Gaga releases a new album, do you think she releases the worst song on the album or does she release the best song on the album as the first single from the album, right? She releases the best song. She's got a new album coming out. She'll release the very best song on the album so that when it comes out, people will hear the song and go, oh, that's brilliant. I love this song. I want to buy the whole album and not just the song, okay? And when they buy the album, they don't think, oh, the rest of the album is no good. They're just pleased that they've got the album. They've got everything. So if you think about it like that, if you think in terms of music, when a band or an artist releases a single, they pick the best singles off the album as a promotional tool to get people to buy the rest of the product, which is the rest of the album. And that's exactly what Egos talked about. They've talked about stripping out the very best golden nuggets of information from your product, putting that into the giveaway just to impress people. Then they go, wow, this is the free stuff because this is what people think. They think, wow. If this is Egos' free stuff, the rest of the stuff must be brilliant. And it's not like ripping people off because all you do is you just make sure that the rest of the information is good as well. You put your best information in the product, but when they go ahead and buy the rest of the information, just make sure that's good as well. Then people won't be disappointed. So that's just another way of looking at it. For anyone listening that's thinking, well, that's a bit strange. I don't understand about giving. Why would I give away the best stuff? That's just a good way of looking at it. The other thing that I wanted to say as well is you said there that a few months ago you would have said to people, the best way to get started in IM is affiliate marketing. Now you're saying, create your own product and then do affiliate marketing as well. The brilliant thing about having your own product on your thank you page is what Egos talking about here is selling your own product on your thank you page. Once people get your free product, they then get taken to thank you page and that's where you offer this product to them as Egos explained. Well, if you've got your own product on this thank you page, you get to keep 100% of the commissions. If it was someone else's product that you were promoting as an affiliate marketer, you'd only be keeping 50% of the commissions. So you're making double the money and it's even better than that because you're establishing yourself in the eyes of the person as a trusted figure of authority. So if you were promoting someone else's product on the thank you page, you'd only be getting 50% of the commissions and you wouldn't be doing anything for your reputation anyway because you'd be building the other person's reputation because it's your product branded with your branding, with your name on it, helping people, teaching people what they want to learn. You're building brand awareness, you're building yourself as a trusted figure of authority and a respected figure with your prospects and with your buyers and of course you're getting double the money because you're promoting it to your own list for 100% of the money. So I just wanted to add that as well in case people hadn't realized that aspect to it as well. But yeah, and then of course you can then, as you say, you can then promote, you can be an affiliate marketer as well, you can just then continue to promote other people's products to your buyers list now you've identified them as buyers. Would you agree that's a bit more to it maybe people hadn't realized, Igor? Yeah, and I mean you don't limit yourself to selling one product of your own and then promoting affiliate products for the rest of your life. You can then promote your other products to your buyers because these people have already tasted your stuff. I mean it's going to be 10 times easier to sell them on another product that you release in the upcoming future than it would be to a complete and total stranger who has either have not heard of you at all, I mean, or have joined your list but never bought for this or that reason, whatever. I mean buyer, I mean you need to treat them special. I mean it's the VIP of your list. You know, like if you were an owner of a restaurant, you probably have a few guys who are VIP, you know, if they come in, you close all the doors, you shut the windows, you turn on some nice music, get the strippers out, you know, make sure they have a good time. Now these are the buyers, okay? I just want to emphasize the point in order for our listeners to get this because this is really, really, really important that they understand what I'm talking about. Brilliant. And just in case anyone is thinking, well what do you mean by treat them right, I guess you're saying they're ego is make sure you send them emails that contain a lot of free information, regular contact from yourself about extra things they can do with that, you know, just like little nuggets of information from yourself and obviously treat them really well, like make sure that you're not spamming them with loads of emails or just want to offer after another. It needs to be, I guess you're saying that it needs to be a mix between sales offers and free content for your buyers. Is that what you're saying? Kind of, yeah. You should send free gifts, preferably, you know, not opt-in gifts. For example, if you want to do something special for them, get buy a new book or a video course and just give it away for free without having them opt-in to get it. Just send them directly to the download page, you know, just as simple as that. Also when I say you need to treat them right, you need to make sure every offer you promote to them is 100% solid, is 100% pure top-notch content that is support of the vendor that you promote is also very quick and very helpful because that's a tough issue in our industry where a lot of people just sell the product and they never support their customers. They don't treat them right and so that leaves a mark on every single purchase they make. Also, as you mentioned, Phil, you must not spend them with daily offers, etc. I mean, keep it at least a couple of days between your promotions because you don't want to get them too angry with you, thinking, wait, I just bought this product, now he says buy this and tomorrow he's going to send me another one. You know, what the hell? This guy's list, he's an Able. So yeah, treat them right, treat them nice, give them bonuses. Yeah, another great tip, whenever you promote an affiliate product to your buyers, you should offer bonuses as an extra incentive or as a gesture saying you're a valid customer of mine and I want to stream the deal even more for you. So buy this $17 product and I'm going to give you $97 value bonus which is a product that has not been released yet that I'm just working on right now or I'm going to finish in touches on, etc. So the point is to make them feel very special, like the bride on a wedding. Perfect. And so for anyone listening right now, just to give them a rule of thumb, would you say that you would only do one offer a week or maybe two offers a week and then interspersed those emails so that they get free gifts and genuine free gifts, not ones I have to opt in and free information in the middle? Or was it just a rule of thumb? If I were to strip down to a formula, I'd say in a seven days period, I'd send out three promotions with paid products, whether it's my product or someone else's. I'd send a couple of personal emails sharing whatever is going on in my life because that will probably straighten out the connection between you and your customer. I mean, sharing personal stuff. I mean, if something's happening to you right now and you just send an email saying I'm kind of, you know, I feel bad right now because I'm going through a rough patch in my life, Sarah. I bet you'll notice, you'll start getting some emails saying, well, don't worry, you know, we're here for you. If you can help, just let me know. And these are customers who pay you money. I mean, you should feel like friends. I mean, if you, for example, I used to go to the hospital a lot because of my really old grandma. She was 98 at the time and so I had to visit the hospital three times in two months. And every time I was in the hospital, I sent out this email saying, I'm sorry, I won't be able to send you any good stuff today because I'm at the hospital again with my grandma. She had another heart attack and etc., etc. And I started getting tons of emails from people saying, well, don't you worry, no, my grandma suffered from a massive heart attack too. She, you know, she recovered really well. People know what they're doing. People send me prayers. I mean, I had a person send me a gift card, you know, in the real mail, in the snail mail saying best wishes to your grandma, say that, you know, people are all over the world praying for her, etc. And this has really, you know, inevitably boosted my sales because now whenever I say something, these guys listen. You know, they see that I'm transparent with them. And so they buy from my links, from my equipment, they buy my products, they respect my word, they listen to my recommendations, etc. So yeah, I'd say three promotions per week, a couple of personal emails and on weekends, you can even give them a nice little freebie. Oh, that's a nice little formula there. So freebie at the weekends, a couple of personal emails and then two or three offers through the week. I'm just really, really glad that you talked about giving, telling people a bit about yourself and doing what you call the personal kind of emails. I'm really glad you mentioned that because those kind of emails, the, what I call relationship building emails, that's the ones where your subscribers, they're going to get to know you. And it's one of the biggest battles that you mentioned about 10 minutes ago. One of the biggest battles we have as internet marketers is trust is because selling over the internet is not face to face. When people stumble upon a sales page, there's no face for them to see. There's no, there's no one they can talk to to find out that it's a real person. And they've got all these fears and worries that they might be giving their money to someone they don't know or scam or whatever they worry about this. And those emails where you just share a little bit about your own life, a little bit about what's going on, just, just makes people see you as a real person. And also just on the plus side as well, you've mentioned it helps yourselves, but it's just, it just makes your job a bit nicer. I mean, when you, when you get those emails from, from your subscribers saying, oh, we're really thinking of you, you're in our thoughts, you're in our prayers. I mean, when you got that greetings card through the post, I mean, that must have really made you day. Yeah. I mean, I did not expect, I did not plan this to happen. But when I noticed that I'm starting to get this kind of feedback, I mean, I immediately started taking measures and treating my list as well as it deserves to be treated. I mean, even my freebie seekers list, not, not weekly, but at least once a month, I sent out a free, a free gift, you know, without an opt-in. And, and, and occasionally I let the PS to my email saying, you can need any help. Just let me know here in my Skype, here's my phone number, here's my email, email me, what, what kind of, what's happened with you, et cetera. And that's what people are looking for because believe it or not, I have, I have a few products out there on the internet. And so oftentimes about once a week, I get an angry person emailing me saying, well, he's, he's probably angry at all of us, you know, in the market. Yes. I've been scammed hundreds of times. If you want to, if you want me to buy a product, here's my phone number, call me. I need someone to talk to. I need, I don't want to pay to a webpage. You know, if you want to sell me on your product, here's my phone number. Talk to me, explain why this is a good fit for me. And so usually if the person's nice enough, if the email is, he's sending me nice enough without any curse words or, you know, blaming me for, for a major disaster in his life for some reason, I pick up the phone, I get on Skype and I call this guy saying, hey, you asked me to call you here I am. I, I freed up 15 minutes of my time just to, to call you and see what's up with you. What exactly are you doing to help with? And here's what, what my product does. And, you know, it feels good, especially because when you connect with your customer, you will be able to craft your offers and products and sales pages in such a laser targeted way that it's insane. I mean, your ability to craft perfect marketing messages will swell up in size. You will become indefinitely, you know, infinitely more, more powerful as a marketer and you will be able to squeeze out 10 times more money out of an offer of other products just by adding a few lines of text to your sales pages because these lines will probably hit the spot for your prospect. They will read them and they will have this aha moment saying that damn it, this guy gets me. I want to work for him. I want to, I want to learn from him and they will buy it. No, I can't, I don't want to say no matter what the price, but you know, the price becomes less of an issue once they know that you get them. That's sorry. Sorry, we done, Igor? I didn't want to interrupt you there. Yeah, yeah, I'm done. There's some real, real quality information there. There's so many things there that I totally agree with. I mean, what you said there about building the trust within relationship, it gets people to buy from you. And the great thing that Igor did there was by phoning. I just want to make an example out of that. Actually, you had, you say that you regularly get people saying to you, oh, no, no, phoned me because I want someone to talk to me. Right. Now, what a lot of marketers would have done, what a lot of marketers would have done is set themselves, stuff that person, forget them if they don't want to buy. I don't, you know, I don't want to know them kind of thing. A lot of people would have taken it. What's the word? They would have taken it defensively. The email. Yeah. It might be a bit, you know, a bit angry. But the person saying, call me and explain what it is. So you did, you've done that. You've, you haven't taken it personally. You've understood that the, the anger is not directed at you. It's directed because they've been scammed in the past and you've phoned them and you've turned it into an opportunity. And that's brilliant. That's the kind of thinking that, that is one of the reasons why you're such a success. And anyone listening, if you can develop that mindset, which is take on board feedback from your customers and from your subscribers and for your prospects. When you get emails that say that someone's not comfortable with something. If you can have the strength of character to say, that person's not comfortable. I'm going to make them comfortable. Instead of going, oh, they're just a winger. They're just to complain and delete the email. If you can have the strength of character to not take it personally and just to say, right, that person needs me to make them more comfortable. They're telling me what's wrong. I'm going to act on it and I'm going to do something about it. That is the kind of thinking that will, that will get you so much further because it's just brilliant. And I love that kind of thinking. So I'm really glad you said about that as well. I just want to add another thing. There's a rule. It's very universal. You can find it in any industry. That's it's the 80-20 rule. And so it goes, it works in marketing as well, which means you will be making approximately 80% of your income, whether via email or whatever. With approximately 20% of your customers because these are the guys who will be no hyperactive buyers. And so when you get a call like that and email like that and respond to it the right way and you manage to turn this guy into a customer after he has been, you know, after he has pissed off at every single market in the world, he will probably most likely become one of those 20% customers who will keep buying from you again and again and again and again in the following few months or maybe a few years. Depends on you actually or whatever topic you're covering because, you know, attention spans are shorter. And so what I'm trying to say is that getting one buyer is more profitable than a free v-seeker because he will be buying again from you. And so even if he only buys a tiny $7 product when he first joins your list, you have natural ability, you know, it's really not that hard and it's pretty common of getting as much as a few hundred bucks out of a single customer and later down the road. Maybe it's going to spread out on the course of a few months or a few weeks, but eventually if he's a proven buyer, he will buy again. And so whenever you get a complaint, you need to tackle it, you need to dissect it, you need to understand what the hell has caused it and make sure you prevent it from happening again in the future. Okay. Absolutely. And you can be, you can be darn sure as well that if, if one person has complained about something, then another 50 people have noticed it, felt the same way and just not said anything because the nature of, of the nature of the average person is they won't tell people when something bothers them. That's just the nature of people. People don't like to complain. So if you get one email about something, don't think, well, that's the only person, you're the only person that's ever complained. So forget it. If that, if one person has complained, there's, you can guarantee there's another 50 people that have also noticed it and just never said anything. So definitely just, as you guys just said, look behind it, find out what's wrong. Look at it as a problem solving exercise rather than a personal attack. That's all it is. It's just something with your business that you need to fix. It's not a personal attack to yourself. And I definitely agree. I just wanted to actually say that something you said about a few minutes back, Igor, that you were talking about people polluting their list by giving them lots and lots of, of, of free gifts that they had to sign in for. So I think it's something that a lot of people with call will know as ad swaps. And it's something that it's a really frequently taught technique of list building. And it's become very popular in the last few years, ad swaps. And just in case anyone that's listening doesn't know what ads are, it's when two marketers decide to tell their own lists about the other person. So for example, if Igor himself decides to an ad swap, he would send his list an email and offering them a free gift from me. And I would send my list an email offering a free gift from him. And we would basically just send each other traffic, send each other subscribers. They'd have to click on the link that go to an opt-in page. They'd give their name and email and they'd then be on the other person's list. It's a way of customer sharing and both people grow. But of course, the problem is, is if you do a lot of this, then your subscribers start to become a bit, a bit annoyed that all the time, whenever they get a free gift from you, they have to give away their email address for it. And all right, giving away an email address is not like paying money, but it still has a cost because if you give away your email address, you know, you're going to get more emails from another marketer. So I wondered if you just wanted to talk about what you found is the best way to do ad swaps and maybe the best level of frequency and kind of the best way to not annoy your subscribers. The last thing is you, as you've already said in the call, you want your subscribers to be, to fill valued. So how can you add swap without annoying them? Simple. You have seven days in a week. Now, let's bear in mind that you need to, to, to get some days and to give away. Let's say you're going to give one, no option gift per week, which leads you six days, right? Let's leave another couple of days for paid product promotions, for affiliate promotions, for click bank launches that are happening for promoting fill products, for more percentage affiliate commission, you know, or, or, or all sorts of stuff. Now, which leaves you approximately three or four days, which you can use for swaps. And I found it to be, you know, a nice little frequency to, to get your list growing without actually annoying too many people. However, when we are talking about annoying people on your list, it's really inevitable. And let me explain, because no matter what you do, you can't, you can't please everybody. And, you know, you can't get to a point where 100% of your list is in love with you. That's just impossible. As I've said, only about 20% of your list will be ever in love with you. And so, I don't, I want you to start swapping approximately three, four times a week, driving traffic to your offers and hopefully selling your product on the back end, making some money, getting those buyers on the list. And again, we're talking about the freebie list, right? You do not swap with your buyer list, never. Because your buyer list is your secret golden asset that no one gets access to. Okay. No ad swaps with buyer list. And so, you swaps about three, four times a week and see how your list responds. Because as much as I'd like to, I can't give you one fits all answer about frequency of emails and ad swaps. I know people who mail three, four times a day. I mean, if you think two or one is too much, I know people who email three or four times a day and they make a good living out of that. And so, you need to test. Start with the three, four apps, ads and swaps per week, a freebie gift and a few promotions, maybe you can then expect to couple email emails per day and more ad swaps or you can go with less emails, more promotions, less ads. You know, you need to figure this out on your own. And I'm telling exactly the same thing to all of my coaching clients, students, customers, no matter who you are. You need to test it out, see how your list responds, see how many unsubscriptions you get per day, see how many clicks you can generate per email, see how many people send complaints over by email. See, I mean, all sorts of stuff. You need to monitor your list. You need to see those stats and you need to figure out what makes them feel most comfortable. If it's, you know, if you can manage sending out couple emails per day, whether it won't be a freebie and another one being a paid offer, why not? If your list is fine with it, if you're getting just a few unsubscribes per day, then, you know, no one says you cannot do it. If you see that's profitable, if you, if you actually see money coming into your bank account thanks to increased frequency, then by all means, do not listen to anyone and just do it. Okay. It's all about testing and it's all about your own list. You really can't have anyone come in and say, this is how it's done about list building and about, sorry, about email frequency because there's only one way to find out and it's to test. And I'm sorry for giving you such a vague, vague answer. But I mean, I hated it myself when I first heard it, but that's the truth. Okay. You need to test it out. No, don't, don't be sorry. That's the perfect answer. And that's the answer I was hoping you were going to say as well. You need to be tested and it's funny actually, because just as you were about to say that, you know, some people that swap three or four times a day, I was thinking the same thing because some people will only do one ad swap a week. Other people, as you say, will do three or four or five or six ad swaps a day, every single day. Some people do that and it's just whatever works for them. It's whatever you're comfortable with and it's crucially how the list respond. And the metrics you obviously that you got mentioned there that you look at just to quickly go over it again for people. The metrics that you want to look at are open rates, un-subscribes, open rates, and the money come in your bank account and complaint emails. So obviously if you do too many ad swaps too quickly, you might get a lot of unsubscribes, so keep an eye on that. You also might start to see that the open rates of your emails goes down and the click-through rates go down as people are getting bombarded with emails. Keep an eye on that as well. And obviously, email complaints, you're going to be looking out for that anyway. So it kind of goes that way. And monetizing as well, that's one of the, just quickly to touch on that as well. That's one of the beauties of ad swaps that maybe people hadn't realized listening is that you can monetize ad swaps with this method that Igor talked about earlier of having a sales offer on the thank you page of your freebie. Obviously when you're ad swapping with your free offer, you're sending new people to your sales page because when they download their free gift, your free gift, you're taking them to your sales page. And so you will be able to monetize these ad swaps instantly because you'll be sending more people to this page. So that's just another little benefit of ad swaps there as well, just in case people hadn't quite realized that. So yeah, really good stuff. Thank you. I appreciate that. I just wanted to. I know that you've also had success with, with solo ads that you got now. I just wondered because here's the thing. Here's my experience of solo ads. Okay. I've had some good ones where I've, I've paid a fair bit of money and I've got a whole new load of prospects and a lot of them have bought. I've had other solo ads that would have paid a lot of money, got a load of prospects and none of them have bought, but at least they got the prospects. And I've had other solo ads where just the experience has been horrible. Where I've had really rubbish leads, nothing like I expected. Um, so obviously I'm doing something wrong because I know that other people are having good success with solo ads. So, and I'm sure that people listening, I mean, maybe they might not know what solo ads are, but I'm sure we can cover that. But I'm sure that people listening kind of maybe have had similar experiences as well, if they have done solo ads. So would you mind just sharing what your experience has taught you about, about buying good solo ads? Um, yeah. Well, first of all, the first thing you do, you don't look at the price. I mean, you do actually, yeah, you do look at the price of the guy who was charging for the solo ad and just to clear things up, you know, for the ones, for the people who don't know what solo ads are. This is basically where you approach a list owner who guarantees a certain amount of clicks that he can deliver and you pay him for these clicks. So, for example, if you were to buy a solo ad for 100 clicks, you'd be charged. Let's say 50 bucks. Okay. So, you know that you're paying half a dollar per click and you're completely backed up by a guarantee. And so first you look at the price. If the guy charges too much for his list, obviously you don't buy because you won't be able to make your money back on the, you know, on selling your own product and promoting further products later. So you're looking for anywhere from 27 to 35 cents per click. That's, I think, personally, the perfect kind of solo ad range, which means if I were to buy a 300 click solo, I'd expect to pay anywhere from 90 to 100 dollars, 110 dollars to this guy. And so that's one. You look at the price, which should be normal, not too high, not too low. Next, you get him to guarantee a certain amount of clicks. And so he sends your ad out and he gets less clicks than he promised. He will either have to resend your ad a couple of more times to match up the clicks or you get your money back. Okay. For example, I have, I'm actually, I won't hide this. I sell solo ads to my list. Of course, before I do, I check out the offer. I make sure it's good, you know, it's good content, et cetera. So I don't send rubbish to my list. But personally, I charge about 40 cents per click because I know my list is good. Okay. And the people who have bought solo ads from me, I make sure they always get their clicks. I give a guarantee, which, which says if by 72 hours from the send out, you do not get the promised amount of clicks I said I would deliver, you get your money, your money back no questions asked. So far I had no refunds. Thank God. But I were to fail to deliver the clicks. I simply go to my PayPal account, issue a refund to this person and say, you know, I did not deliver, you know, here's your money back. Now that's the second thing. One is you look for the price. The second you look for a guarantee. Third, you try to get this person to give you a few testimonials and the people who use your service. Now this is really, really important because I mean, he can't fake the price. He can't give you a guarantee, but testimonials from people you know, or personally, or maybe you've heard about, or, I don't know, whatever you can find about this person will be helpful. For example, on the warrior forum where I sell my solo at service, I have, I actually make every customer who buys a solo go back to the thread and leave me a testimony. Whether good or bad, I just say the truth, whatever you feel. And so far I have four pages worth of great testimonials, which helps me sell my solo as such high prices, for instance, per click. Now, that's what you need to look for. That's why if you're just getting started, if you listen to this interview, whether you got it from Phil or me, get in contact with me or Phil, again, or both of us and ask around, who do we recommend? In that case, you won't burn on your first solo. Okay, we will recommend people who we have personally used to deliver good quality traffic. Now, that's as far as picking a solo delivery guide goes. Now, how do you make sure it converts? Well, I can say all the solo ads I've done have been flawless. No, I have some as you mentioned, Phil, I had some solos that delivered tons of options and tons of sales where I actually made all my money back on the front end. I had solos that delivered tons of options and no sales at all. I mean, like 300 options and no sales. And I had solos that delivered twice as low the option rate as I'm used to. And little to no sales. And so these are the ones I hate the most, where you get twice, you know, you get half the option rate and you get no sales at all. This is a real bummer. How do you prevent it? Again, you've got to test out. And if you're not sure about this particular guy or girl who sells you the solo, what you can do is you can run a small test, you know, get him to send you, let's say 100 clicks for 30 bucks, which is decent kind of price. And with 100 clicks, I think it's enough to see what kind of traffic this person has. Now, if you make a sale or two, if you will get your every option rate, then you can go ahead, wait for another week and get a larger solo. If not, well, you just move on. I mean, and if the solo you're running this guy or girl is successful, the next step is to ask this person if he buys solos. And if he does, ask where where he gets them. And I get this quite a lot where a person buys a solo for me. And then he says, actually, I'm looking to buy more solos. Can you recommend anyone? And I go and recommend the folks who I buy solos from because if my traffic's good, you know, it means that the traffic sources I use are good. OK, and so he can expect same kind of conversions or at least similar from the person I recommend without being afraid that he's going to get burnt. I hope that, you know, I hope this again covers what you're trying to get out of me. Written by the people you know, which is the best thing. OK, that's a really good indication of someone's reputation. If they've been thanked a lot, that's it. It's not 100 percent, but it's a good indication. It was definitely you got his and so has the best. But yeah, joking aside, if you can see a thread where there's one person after another thanking them and saying this is great, it's a good indication. Now, I'm not going to say that it's 100 percent definite because it's possible for someone to fake that. OK, it's possible for someone to put an advert and register under a load of different names or get friends to say stuff. It's possible for someone to do that. But I'm going to say I don't think it's very likely. And I think it's much more likely that if you have a thread with loads and loads of good comments with someone that's been thanked a lot, it's a really good strong indication. And once you've had your first and as Igor said, you know, by a small one, I'm terrible. This is one of the reasons why I've had some bad results in the past. I am terrible for bargains. I love a bargain, right? And I've been tempted into bargains before. And here's what happens is Igor was saying that he pays between 25 and 35 cents a click and you might be thinking, well, why does it vary? Well, what a lot of listeners will do is they will give you a discount for the more clicks you buy. So you might pay $50 for 100 clicks, but you might only pay $70, $75 for 200 clicks. And, you know, you might pay $300 for 1000 clicks or 20, 270 for 1000 clicks. The price per click comes down the more you buy. That's just the industry standard. That's the way it's done. Obviously to incentivize people to buy more. Yeah. It's a bulk discount. Now I'm terrible. I love a bargain. So what I've done in the past is I've gone, right, what's the maximum number of clicks this person will sell me 1000. They'll do it for 270. That's better value than buying, you know, 10, 100 clicks. I'll just do that. And then surprise, surprise, I get really poor results. So it's definitely better, especially if it's the first time you use someone or if you don't have a testimony about them or if you don't really know them. Then just try a little, you know, $30 ad swap or a $50 ad swap, something that's not going to break the bank. Anyone listening right now that doesn't have funds to do solo ads? Well, stick with your business. Keep doing the ad swaps. Keep sending out the offer emails in a good way to your list and put the money, any money that you make from instant marketing. When you first get started, the first few dollars that you start to make, put that into a pot or put that into a certain bank account, a different bank account and don't spend it. Right. And here's the reasoning. You so many people when they come into into marketing, they're desperate to make the first dollar and they really want them. They really want to start making the money. And what you've got to do is you've got to resist the temptation to spend it as soon as you make it. And here's what my philosophy. You were doing okay without it before you were, you were, you were eating. You were eating and you were paying the bills and you had a roof over your head before you made that $20 or that $10. So put that into a pot and save, wait until you've made 50 or 100 and then you can buy your first solo ads. Okay. So I, I, I know that most people prefer free traffic when you first get started. That's why we're telling people about ad swaps and about other list building methods, but definitely put the money aside as you make it, resist the temptation to treat yourself, resist the temptation to spend it straight away, put it to good use and, and then you, you're going to start to, to make money. And of course, if it was a good solo ad, you're going to make a good percentage of the money back. Maybe, maybe if it's a really good one once in a while, you'll make all the money back straight away. And even if you don't, you've got a whole new, new prospects, which is why people do it. So you'll make the money back. Maybe not today, but within a week. Yeah. Further down the road, further down the road. And of course, if, you know, if you're having problems with your, with your sales products converting, you know, then just, you know, you can, you can make little changes to improve the conversions. That's a different thing we can talk about. But yes, no, some really good tips there for, for avoiding the scammers. And just a final thing, if the price is really low, if it seems too good to be true, beware, beware, if it's like 15, 20 cents a click, then I mean, unless it's huge, unless it's like, you know, 10,000 clicks for, No, unless, unless this guy actually gives you a real reason. So I ran this quick special, but I gave them a reason. I told them that they can get a really cheap solo with me. Just this one's two spots, you know, get a fat. And so I had eight people approach me, but only two could get it. And so if someone gives you a really solid reason, at least, you know, this makes it like half believable. I mean, you can't go and buy a solo, but talking from experience here, once I went for a solo that was 500 clicks for 100 bucks. And it was a complete disaster. I mean, a real fiasco. And so really, if someone offers you a really, really, really cheap solo for no reason whatsoever, just stay away. But if he can give you a solid reason, for example, if he says, well, I kind of need quick cash right now and I don't want one of the affiliate products to promote. Oh, I mean, here's the thing that most people never realized. And personally, I never realized it myself until just a few months back. Um, when, when someone sends out a solo ad, for example, for a few hundred clicks, first of all, it does not mean he sends out the set to his whole list. He may be sending out just a segment of his list because he may be able to deliver way more clicks than you can think he can. And so, uh, when you buy the solo ad in the second time, this guy, he knows that he sent out to this segment or to that segment. Now it's not the same 50 to 100 people opening each email. Um, you will get some subscribers that, you know, always, always open your emails, but most of the time it's going to be different people because they've got their own lives, you know, one day they might be able to open your email because they haven't, they're not very busy. Another day they might not. So if you're consistently getting around a hundred clicks for email, it's not the same 100 people. If, um, people, for people listening that they may not be aware of this, but, um, using services such as bud URL or bitly, um, you can, you can, you can know with 100. Well, okay. They're not 100% accurate, but you can know with, you know, to within 99% of the accuracy, you can know how many clicks that person sent. You don't have to just take their word for it or look at how many new subscribers you got that day. You can know for a fact how many clicks they did by using, um, a link tracking service such as bitly or bud URL, or if you have a WordPress blog, uh, there's a plugin called pretty link that I use. It's a free plugin. Um, in fact, you don't actually need to have a blog. You just need to have a website with WordPress installed and you can run it off that. I use pretty link and it's free and, uh, it allows you to do multiple links to this multiple different trackings to the same click, which is an advantage over bitly. Bitly doesn't let you do that. The, the, the model that most people have been doing for the last few years and internet marketing, which Mike Phil same kind of made popular, which is the giveaway of free gift to get a subscriber model. Okay. That is like, um, you know, when you walk through town, you walk through, you go to the shopping mall or you walk through town and you get those people giving out flyers, they're giving out free flyers and you, yeah, how, how many people take the flyer, not many people. They, uh, they, they, they just keep on walking, right? We're trying to give away a free gift. There's a bit like trying to give away a flyer because you're trying to give this free thing to someone. They may not necessarily be interested. And crucially, it's one on one. You're, you're, you're trying to give your free gift to each individual, uh, prospect. Okay. And anyone that's ever used a JV giveaways will know that if you enter a giveaway, you put your list, your gift on the giveaway. You don't get great results from giveaways because it's particularly when you're just starting to, you know, for all sorts of reasons, but it's hard. Now I like to look at affiliates as, um, in business, when you, when, if you, if you want, if you, if you say, for example, you, you were a manufacturer of, of, of hammers, right? If you wanted to sell a lot of hammers, right? You wouldn't walk around the streets, um, trying to find people to sell hammers to individually, you'd go to, you'd go to shops and the kind of shops you'd go to would be the bigger ones. So you try and get your hammer into the, uh, a big chain of, of, uh, of hardware shops right across the country and across the world. And you'd spend more time trying to do that than you would try to get it into all the little independent shops. Okay. Affiliates are like shops. They're like, um, shops of customers. Okay. And if I'm not making sense, tell me, but when you, when you get an affiliate on side, they supply you with customers wholesale. So instead of you trying to recruit the customers individually, you recruit the affiliates individually and then the affiliates send you customers in bulk. A bit like, um, a chain of stores would be able to sell your product for you in bulk. Yeah. Um, I got it from, I'm sure that all over the world, people have the, the, the, the show, the apprentice. Um, it's all over the world's franchise all over the world. And there's usually a task, um, in each series where the, the contestants have to sell a product and some of them go to pitch to the wholesalers and some of them go to the small shops and some of them try and sell in the street. And you get the most orders by going to the big guys. So the bigger an affiliate, you can get them the more luck you'll have. The more you incentivize it, the more luck you'll have. And then even you can go one step beyond and you can bulk recruit affiliates by, I mean, I don't want to go into too much detail because we'll just go on forever, but you can have a two tier affiliate program whereby you pay affiliates a commission for referring other affiliates. And then you're getting wholesale supply of affiliates to wholesale supply customers. That's a bit kind of advanced. Sorry, say that again, but you need software for that. Yeah. Yeah. Okay. Yeah. I mean, I'm going a bit further down the line, but it's just, it's another strategy for people. So if they want to take it to the next step. So, uh, just, it's wanted to mention it. Um, right.
